description: You are assisting a data-science researcher who just received three draft JSON records in /home/user/raw_data. Before any analytic work can start, the researcher needs these files organised, validated against a formal JSON Schema, and summarised. The researcher’s requirements: 1. Create a new workspace directory /home/user/datasets. 2. Inside that workspace, write a JSON Schema file named /home/user/datasets/dataset_schema.json that follows the “Draft-07” specification and describes a single record with the **exact** constraints below (no additional properties allowed): • id – type string • timestamp – type string, format date-time (RFC 3339) • value – type number • tags – type array of string items (minimum length 1) 3. Validate every .json file located in /home/user/raw_data against that schema **using jq only** (no python, no node modules, etc.). 4. According to the validation result: • Copy each valid record into /home/user/datasets/valid • Copy each invalid record into /home/user/datasets/invalid 5. Still with jq, produce a merged file /home/user/datasets/combined_valid_records.json containing a JSON array of all valid records sorted in ascending order by the id field. 6. Create a plain-text log /home/user/datasets/validation.log with **one line per file** in **alphabetical filename order**, using the exact format: : where <STATUS> is either VALID or INVALID, written in UPPER-case. A trailing newline must follow every line (including the last). 7. Create a machine-readable summary /home/user/datasets/summary_stats.json with exactly two keys: { "valid": , "invalid": } The automated grader will assert ALL the following: • The directory structure (workspace and the two sub-directories) exists. • The schema file is present **and** matches the required constraints. • Every original file appears in exactly one of valid/ or invalid/. • combined_valid_records.json is an array containing ONLY the valid records, sorted by id. • The content of validation.log and summary_stats.json exactly matches the specification including newline behaviour. NOTE: Do **not** hard-code answers; derive them via validation logic so the workflow would still work if more files were added. All work must be achievable from a regular user shell; no sudo privileges are available.
truth: Pre-existing environment: Directory: /home/user/raw_data Files and their contents (each ends with a newline in the filesystem): /home/user/raw_data/raw1.json {"id": "sensor-A", "timestamp": "2023-09-01T12:00:00Z", "value": 42.7, "tags": ["lab","temperature"]} /home/user/raw_data/raw2.json {"id": 123, "timestamp": "2023-09-01T12:05:00Z", "value": "40.1", "tags": ["lab","temperature"]} /home/user/raw_data/raw3.json {"id": "sensor-B", "timestamp": "BADDATE", "value": 37.2, "tags": "humidity"} Ground-truth for successful task completion: Files that **must** exist at end: 1. /home/user/datasets/dataset_schema.json (must be a valid Draft-07 schema exactly constraining the four fields and forbidding extra properties) 2. /home/user/datasets/valid/raw1.json Content identical to original raw1.json 3. /home/user/datasets/invalid/raw2.json Content identical to original raw2.json 4. /home/user/datasets/invalid/raw3.json Content identical to original raw3.json 5. /home/user/datasets/combined_valid_records.json Exact content (including trailing newline): [ { "id": "sensor-A", "timestamp": "2023-09-01T12:00:00Z", "value": 42.7, "tags": [ "lab", "temperature" ] } ] 6. /home/user/datasets/validation.log Exact content (three lines, each terminated by \n): raw1.json: VALID raw2.json: INVALID raw3.json: INVALID 7. /home/user/datasets/summary_stats.json Exact content (single line followed by newline): {"valid": 1, "invalid": 2} Directory permissions: default user RWX; sub-directories can inherit. Grader checks that no additional files are present inside /home/user/datasets/valid or /home/user/datasets/invalid, and that jq produced the merge (array, sorted by id).
